description_long: This object is a vintage-style radio receiver with a rectangular wooden cabinet featuring a rich, dark brown finish. The front panel displays a large, fabric-covered speaker grille above a control section that includes two prominent round knobs, a horizontal tuning display, and several rectangular buttons. The brand name 'Arizona' is visible on the upper right of the speaker grille. The sides of the radio are smooth and unadorned, while the back panel is a light-colored perforated surface, likely for ventilation and sound resonance. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century tabletop radios, combining both functional and decorative elements.
description: A vintage wooden radio receiver with a fabric speaker grille, control knobs, and a tuning display, featuring the brand 'Arizona' on the front.
description_view_0: Front view showing the speaker grille, control knobs, tuning display, and the 'Arizona' brand.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the smooth, dark wooden surface with no visible controls or features.
description_view_2: Rear view featuring a light-colored, perforated panel likely for ventilation and sound output.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the plain wooden surface.
